paper_title: Sediment Core Analysis in Shanxi Province

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from lakes in Shanxi Province and preparing them for chemical analysis.

steps.description: Sediment cores were retrieved from lakes GH, MY, DL, ZS, and KL using a gravity core with a 90-millimeter-diameter polycarbonate tube.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores from specified lakes using gravity core

steps.description: Sediment cores were sectioned at 1.0 cm intervals, weighed, freeze-dried, homogenized, ground to a powder smaller than 63 µm, and stored in plastic bags.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediment cores by sectioning, drying, homogenizing, and grinding

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing mercury concentrations in sediment cores using cold vapor-atomic fluorescence spectrometry and direct mercury analysis.

steps.description: Samples were digested using 8 mL of aqua regia in polypropylene DigiTUBE on a hot plate at 100°C for 1.5 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples with aqua regia on hot plate

steps.description: Mercury concentrations were measured using cold vapor-atomic fluorescence spectrometry after reduction with SnCl2.
Quality control was implemented by digesting and analyzing analytical blanks and standard reference materials.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality control with blanks and reference materials

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing concentrations of heavy metals in sediment samples using ICP-AES and ICP-MS.

steps.description: Sediment samples were digested in Teflon tubes using a combination of hydrochloric acid, perchloric acid, hydrofluoric acid, and nitric acid at 145-165°C.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples with acid mixture in Teflon tubes

steps.description: Concentrations of major elements were determined using ICP-AES, while other metals were determined using ICP-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze metal concentrations using ICP-AES and ICP-MS

steps.description: Duplicate measurements and standard geological reference materials were employed as controls.
steps.searchable_summary: validate analysis with duplicates and reference materials

methods.description: Method for determining the chronology of sediment cores using radioisotope activities and the CRS model.

steps.description: Activities of 137Cs, 210Pb, and 226Ra in sediment cores were detected with a low-background germanium detector.
steps.searchable_summary: detect radioisotope activities using germanium detector

steps.description: Chronologies of sediment cores were determined using the CRS model in combination with 137Cs.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sediment chronology using CRS model
